args.prompt:
Design a practical Python program that detects scam text messages and marks them as junk using a hybrid rule-based + Gemini LLM approach. Requirements:
1) Include a brief architecture overview in comments or docstring.
2) Implement a Python classifier that uses heuristics first (keywords, URLs, urgency, spoofed numbers, OTP/request patterns) and then calls Gemini for borderline cases.
3) Use gemini-pro with temperature 0.5 and max_output_tokens 1500. Show exactly how these settings are applied in code.
4) Include Gemini API prompt/template examples for scam detection classification, returning JSON with fields like label, confidence, reasons.
5) Include functions to flag/move messages to junk in a mailbox or message store, with pluggable adapters/stubs for IMAP mailbox, Android/SMS store, or a database-backed message store.
6) Include safety, evaluation, and deployment notes in comments/docstring.
7) Make the code self-contained and runnable as a reference implementation, with placeholders where external credentials/APIs are needed.

Language: Python.
Output file: /tmp/scam_sms_junk_classifier.py

test_cases.input: Design a new interaction interface for the chating robot.

arguments.user_personas: Young adults (18-35) who frequently use messaging apps, prefer quick and intuitive UI, comfortable with emojis and GIFs.
arguments.interaction_modes: Voice commands, text chat, gesture recognition.
arguments.design_principles: Minimalist design, high contrast for readability, responsive layout, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).
arguments.usability_testing: A/B testing with 50 users, heuristic evaluation, think-aloud protocol.
arguments.output_artifacts: Interactive prototype, persona documentation, usability test report.

tools_called.name: UIUX_Expert

args.user_personas: Primary: busy consumers using a home or companion chatting robot for quick assistance, entertainment, reminders, and companionship. Secondary: older adults needing simple voice-first interaction, and accessibility users who benefit from multimodal input/output. Tertiary: caregivers/household admins who configure preferences, safety, and routines.
args.interaction_modes: Hybrid voice + text chat, with optional touch screen, physical quick-action buttons, and ambient glanceable feedback. Voice is default for low-friction use; text is preferred in noisy/private contexts.
args.design_principles: Conversational clarity, low cognitive load, progressive disclosure, privacy by default, error tolerance, consistency across modes, accessible multimodal design, and fast recoverability. Keep the robot’s conversational state visible and explainable.
args.prototyping_tools: Figma for screen flows and component states; optional interactive prototype with voice-state overlays and motion tokens.
args.usability_testing: Task-based usability tests for first-time setup, switching input modes, recovering from failed recognition, and completing common requests. Heuristic evaluation for clarity, accessibility checks (contrast, focus order, screen reader labels), and comparative testing against voice-only and text-only flows.
args.output_artifacts: Concise product UX proposal, information architecture, screen layout, interaction-state map, text wireframe, and prioritized feature set ready for stakeholder review.

- Parameter types: All parameters are strings in both expected and actual calls, no type mismatches.
- No extra or missing parameters.
Overall, the actual call matches the expected call in parameter names and types perfectly, with no violations of evaluation rules.

test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: Modern business website/web app supporting marketing pages, authenticated user workflows, content management, transactional features, analytics, and admin operations. The system should be modular, API-driven, secure, scalable, and maintainable, with separation of presentation, application logic, data storage, and integrations. Include support for public-facing pages, user accounts, role-based access, form submissions, notifications, reporting, and future feature expansion.
args.technology_stack: Option A (balanced modern web app): Frontend: Next.js/React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS; Backend: Node.js (NestJS or Express) or Next.js API routes for lighter workloads; Database: PostgreSQL; Cache/Queue: Redis; Authentication: Auth.js/NextAuth or managed IdP; Hosting: Vercel/AWS/GCP. Option B (enterprise): Frontend: React/Next.js; Backend: Java/Spring Boot or .NET; Database: PostgreSQL or MySQL; Cache: Redis; Search: Elasticsearch/OpenSearch; Hosting: Kubernetes or managed app platform. Option C (rapid delivery): Frontend: CMS-driven or low-code frontend with React components; Backend: headless CMS + serverless functions; Database: managed SQL; Hosting: managed cloud platform. Common supporting tools: CDN, object storage, observability stack, CI/CD, IaC, analytics platform.
args.hardware_requirements: Cloud-first deployment. For MVP: 2 vCPU application instances, 4-8 GB RAM, managed PostgreSQL with automated backups, Redis cache (small), object storage for media, CDN for static assets, autoscaling enabled. For growth: horizontally scalable app tier, read replicas for database, separate worker nodes for background jobs, centralized logging/monitoring, WAF and DDoS protection. For high traffic: container orchestration, multi-AZ database, queue workers, load balancer, and performance testing environment.
args.interaction_interface: Responsive web UI optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile. Support intuitive navigation, clear calls to action, accessible forms, authenticated dashboards, admin panels, and contextual feedback states. Consider progressive enhancement and optional mobile-first design patterns. If needed, include design system components for consistency.
args.test_optimization_plan: Implement automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests; accessibility testing (WCAG checks); performance budgets for Core Web Vitals; load testing for peak traffic scenarios; security scanning in CI/CD; user analytics and funnel tracking; A/B testing for high-impact UX changes; continuous monitoring of uptime, errors, latency, and conversion metrics.
